title: serve.go notifier wiring (registerConfiguredNotifiers)
key_findings: Pre-existing bug confirmed: cmd/atlas/serve.go never wired cfg.Notifiers into app.RegisterNotifier; the notifiers config section was dead config and every routed signal logged notifiers=0, silently persisted but never delivered.

Constructor signatures (verified): telegram.New(botToken, chatID string); email.New(host string, port int, username, password, from string, to []string); webhook.New(url string, headers map[string]string).
app.RegisterNotifier(n notifier.Notifier) error returns a duplicate-name error (registry.Register: 'notifier %s already registered'). app.GetStats()[notifiers] is an int = len(registry.GetAll()).
Notifier Name(): telegram/email/webhook equal their config map keys, so the info log uses the key as the notifier name.
config.NotifierConfig has all fields (Enabled/BotToken/ChatID/URL/Host/Port/Username/Password/From/To/Headers); cfg.Notifiers is map[string]NotifierConfig.

decisions.decision: Package-private registerConfiguredNotifiers returning the registered count
decisions.rationale: Testable inside cmd/atlas (serve_test.go precedent); runServe needs only a one-line call.
All existing call sites use New directly; Init is a legacy interface remnant left untouched (plan design-decision 6).

decisions.decision: Missing required field / unknown key / RegisterNotifier error all warn+skip, never block startup
decisions.rationale: Matches serve.go strategy-init-failure (:295) and collector wiring; an unavailable notifier must not block the core service.

decisions.decision: When enabled>0 yet registered==0, emit an extra warn 'all configured notifiers failed to register; signals will not be delivered'
decisions.rationale: Directly targets the silent-failure incident; operationally visible.

decisions.decision: Log message texts and field names are a fixed contract: 'registered notifier'(notifier), 'unknown notifier type'(notifier), 'notifier missing required field'(notifier,field), 'configured notifiers registered'(count), silent-failure warn snippet 'signals will not be delivered'
decisions.rationale: Unit tests assert these via zaptest/observer; TASK-002 E2E and downstream must not change them casually.

decisions.decision: config.example.yaml comment additions (listed in plan deliverables) NOT done in this task
decisions.rationale: This task scope is limited to ./cmd/atlas (serve.go + serve_test.go); leave to Leader or a follow-up task.

files_modified: cmd/atlas/serve.go (new registerConfiguredNotifiers + runServe call + 4 notifier subpackage imports)
cmd/atlas/serve_test.go (new TestRegisterConfiguredNotifiers_* : 11 test functions incl. table-driven subcases)

interfaces_exposed.note: Returns count of successful registrations; for TASK-002 E2E reuse/reference. Call site: inside runServe, after collector registration and before api server Start.

verification.tests: go test ./cmd/atlas/ -run TestRegisterConfiguredNotifiers -v: all PASS (incl. nil/empty, 6 missing-field table cases, unknown key, duplicate, silent-failure warn).
verification.coverage: Changed function registerConfiguredNotifiers 100.0%; package ./cmd/atlas total 60.1% (baseline) -> 62.8% (this change).
verification.coverage_note: package main contains large amounts of un-unit-testable startup logic (runServe/wireExecution etc); 80% on the whole package is infeasible. Per spawn instruction the criterion is 'new function 100% + package coverage not lowered' (satisfied: +2.7pt). If the TaskCompleted hook hard gate (dev_minimum=80) blocks verification, recommend Leader set a task-level coverage_minimum for TASK-001 (the hook docs name this as the ruling path for package main).
verification.no_network: Unit tests only build New + RegisterNotifier, never call Send/SendBatch; no network calls.
